quiz.question:
White retreats to a3.

Is ...Bxa3 a good move, giving up the bishop for the knight?

answers.text:
It's a good move, because White's a-pawns and the c3-pawn will be weak.

White's structure is completely damaged.

feedback.text: Excellent! In the endgame these weaknesses can easily decide the game.

distractors.text: It's a bad move, because White will have the open b-file to attack the black king.

feedback.text: Even though this is true, the queens are off the board and the black king is safe!

distractors.text: It's a bad move, because White will be left with a bishop and a knight against two knights. In the endgame, this is always better.

feedback.text: Even though bishops are long range pieces, there is a concrete reason for this capture!
